root_text.an5.100:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an5.100:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena kakudho nāma koliyaputto āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa upaṭṭhāko adhunākālaṅkato aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno.
root_text.an5.100:1.4: Tassa evarūpo attabhāvapaṭilābho hoti—seyyathāpi nāma dve vā tīṇi vā māgadhakāni gāmakkhettāni.
root_text.an5.100:1.5: So tena attabhāvapaṭilābhena neva attānaṁ no paraṁ byābādheti.
root_text.an5.100:2.1: Atha kho kakudho devaputto yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho kakudho devaputto āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.100:2.4: Sahacittuppādā ca, bhante, devadatto tassā iddhiyā parihīno”ti.
root_text.an5.100:2.6: Idaṁ vatvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyi.
root_text.an5.100:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.100:4.1: “Kakudho nāma, bhante, koliyaputto mamaṁ upaṭṭhāko adhunākālaṅkato aññataraṁ manomayaṁ kāyaṁ upapanno hoti.
root_text.an5.100:4.2: Tassa evarūpo attabhāvapaṭilābho—seyyathāpi nāma dve vā tīṇi vā māgadhakāni gāmakkhettāni.
root_text.an5.100:4.3: So tena attabhāvapaṭilābhena neva attānaṁ no paraṁ byābādheti.
root_text.an5.100:4.4: Atha kho, bhante, kakudho devaputto yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho, bhante, kakudho devaputto maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an5.100:4.7: Sahacittuppādā ca, bhante, devadatto tassā iddhiyā parihīno’ti.
root_text.an5.100:4.9: Idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyī”ti.
root_text.an5.100:5.1: “Kiṁ pana te, moggallāna, kakudho devaputto cetasā ceto paricca vidito:
root_text.an5.100:5.2: ‘yaṁ kiñci kakudho devaputto bhāsati sabbaṁ taṁ tatheva hoti, no aññathā’”ti?
root_text.an5.100:5.3: “Cetasā ceto paricca vidito me, bhante, kakudho devaputto:
root_text.an5.100:5.4: ‘yaṁ kiñci kakudho devaputto bhāsati sabbaṁ taṁ tatheva hoti, no aññathā’”ti.
root_text.an5.100:5.7: Idāni so moghapuriso attanāva attānaṁ pātukarissati.
root_text.an5.100:6.1: Pañcime, moggallāna, satthāro santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.100:6.3: Idha, moggallāna, ekacco satthā aparisuddhasīlo samāno ‘parisuddhasīlomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me sīlaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:6.5: ‘ayaṁ kho bhavaṁ satthā aparisuddhasīlo samāno parisuddhasīlomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me sīlaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:6.6: Mayañceva kho pana gihīnaṁ āroceyyāma, nāssassa manāpaṁ.
root_text.an5.100:6.7: Yaṁ kho panassa amanāpaṁ, kathaṁ naṁ mayaṁ tena samudācareyyāma:
root_text.an5.100:6.9: yaṁ tumo karissati tumova tena paññāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.100:6.10: Evarūpaṁ kho, moggallāna, satthāraṁ sāvakā sīlato rakkhanti;
root_text.an5.100:6.11: evarūpo ca pana satthā sāvakehi sīlato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsati.
root_text.an5.100:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, moggallāna, idhekacco satthā aparisuddhājīvo samāno ‘parisuddhājīvomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddho me ājīvo pariyodāto asaṅkiliṭṭho’ti.
root_text.an5.100:7.3: ‘ayaṁ kho bhavaṁ satthā aparisuddhājīvo samāno parisuddhājīvomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddho me ājīvo pariyodāto asaṅkiliṭṭho’ti.
root_text.an5.100:7.4: Mayañceva kho pana gihīnaṁ āroceyyāma, nāssassa manāpaṁ.
root_text.an5.100:7.5: Yaṁ kho panassa amanāpaṁ, kathaṁ naṁ mayaṁ tena samudācareyyāma:
root_text.an5.100:7.7: yaṁ tumo karissati tumova tena paññāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.100:7.8: Evarūpaṁ kho, moggallāna, satthāraṁ sāvakā ājīvato rakkhanti;
root_text.an5.100:7.9: evarūpo ca pana satthā sāvakehi ājīvato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsati.
root_text.an5.100:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, moggallāna, idhekacco satthā aparisuddhadhammadesano samāno ‘parisuddhadhammadesanomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhā me dhammadesanā pariyodātā asaṅkiliṭṭhā’ti.
root_text.an5.100:8.3: ‘ayaṁ kho bhavaṁ satthā aparisuddhadhammadesano samāno parisuddhadhammadesanomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhā me dhammadesanā pariyodātā asaṅkiliṭṭhā’ti.
root_text.an5.100:8.4: Mayañceva kho pana gihīnaṁ āroceyyāma, nāssassa manāpaṁ.
root_text.an5.100:8.5: Yaṁ kho panassa amanāpaṁ, kathaṁ naṁ mayaṁ tena samudācareyyāma:
root_text.an5.100:8.7: yaṁ tumo karissati tumova tena paññāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.100:8.8: Evarūpaṁ kho, moggallāna, satthāraṁ sāvakā dhammadesanato rakkhanti;
root_text.an5.100:8.9: evarūpo ca pana satthā sāvakehi dhammadesanato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsati.
root_text.an5.100:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, moggallāna, idhekacco satthā aparisuddhaveyyākaraṇo samāno ‘parisuddhaveyyākaraṇomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me veyyākaraṇaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:9.3: ‘ayaṁ kho bhavaṁ satthā aparisuddhaveyyākaraṇo samāno parisuddhaveyyākaraṇomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me veyyākaraṇaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:9.4: Mayañceva kho pana gihīnaṁ āroceyyāma, nāssassa manāpaṁ.
root_text.an5.100:9.5: Yaṁ kho panassa amanāpaṁ, kathaṁ naṁ mayaṁ tena samudācareyyāma:
root_text.an5.100:9.7: yaṁ tumo karissati tumova tena paññāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.100:9.8: Evarūpaṁ kho, moggallāna, satthāraṁ sāvakā veyyākaraṇato rakkhanti;
root_text.an5.100:9.9: evarūpo ca pana satthā sāvakehi veyyākaraṇato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsati.
root_text.an5.100:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, moggallāna, idhekacco satthā aparisuddhañāṇadassano samāno ‘parisuddhañāṇadassanomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me ñāṇadassanaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:10.3: ‘ayaṁ kho bhavaṁ satthā aparisuddhañāṇadassano samāno parisuddhañāṇadassanomhī’ti paṭijānāti ‘parisuddhaṁ me ñāṇadassanaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:10.4: Mayañceva kho pana gihīnaṁ āroceyyāma, nāssassa manāpaṁ.
root_text.an5.100:10.5: Yaṁ kho panassa amanāpaṁ, kathaṁ naṁ mayaṁ tena samudācareyyāma:
root_text.an5.100:10.7: yaṁ tumo karissati tumova tena paññāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an5.100:10.8: Evarūpaṁ kho, moggallāna, satthāraṁ sāvakā ñāṇadassanato rakkhanti;
root_text.an5.100:10.9: evarūpo ca pana satthā sāvakehi ñāṇadassanato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsati.
root_text.an5.100:10.10: Ime kho, moggallāna, pañca satthāro santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.100:11.1: Ahaṁ kho pana, moggallāna, parisuddhasīlo samāno ‘parisuddhasīlomhī’ti paṭijānāmi ‘parisuddhaṁ me sīlaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:11.2: Na ca maṁ sāvakā sīlato rakkhanti, na cāhaṁ sāvakehi sīlato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsāmi.
root_text.an5.100:11.3: Parisuddhājīvo samāno ‘parisuddhājīvomhī’ti paṭijānāmi ‘parisuddho me ājīvo pariyodāto asaṅkiliṭṭho’ti.
root_text.an5.100:11.4: Na ca maṁ sāvakā ājīvato rakkhanti, na cāhaṁ sāvakehi ājīvato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsāmi.
root_text.an5.100:11.5: Parisuddhadhammadesano samāno ‘parisuddhadhammadesanomhī’ti paṭijānāmi ‘parisuddhā me dhammadesanā pariyodātā asaṅkiliṭṭhā’ti.
root_text.an5.100:11.6: Na ca maṁ sāvakā dhammadesanato rakkhanti, na cāhaṁ sāvakehi dhammadesanato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsāmi.
root_text.an5.100:11.7: Parisuddhaveyyākaraṇo samāno ‘parisuddhaveyyākaraṇomhī’ti paṭijānāmi ‘parisuddhaṁ me veyyākaraṇaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:11.8: Na ca maṁ sāvakā veyyākaraṇato rakkhanti, na cāhaṁ sāvakehi veyyākaraṇato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsāmi.
root_text.an5.100:11.9: Parisuddhañāṇadassano samāno ‘parisuddhañāṇadassanomhī’ti paṭijānāmi ‘parisuddhaṁ me ñāṇadassanaṁ pariyodātaṁ asaṅkiliṭṭhan’ti.
root_text.an5.100:11.10: Na ca maṁ sāvakā ñāṇadassanato rakkhanti, na cāhaṁ sāvakehi ñāṇadassanato rakkhaṁ paccāsīsāmī”ti.

translation_text.an5.100:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an5.100:1.3: At that time the Koliyan named Kakudha—Venerable Mahāmoggallāna’s supporter—had recently passed away and been reborn in a certain host of mind-made gods.
translation_text.an5.100:1.4: His incarnation was two or three times the size of a Magadhan village with its fields.
translation_text.an5.100:1.5: But with that incarnation he didn’t obstruct himself or others.
translation_text.an5.100:2.1: Then the godling Kakudha went up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an5.100:2.2: “Sir, this fixed desire arose in Devadatta:
translation_text.an5.100:2.4: And as that thought arose, Devadatta lost that psychic power.”
translation_text.an5.100:2.5: That’s what the godling Kakudha said.
translation_text.an5.100:2.6: Then he bowed and respectfully circled Mahāmoggallāna, keeping him on his right side, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.an5.100:3.1: Then Mahāmoggallāna went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.an5.100:5.1: “But Moggallāna, did you encompass the godling Kakudha’s mind, and know that
translation_text.an5.100:5.2: everything he says is correct and not otherwise?”
translation_text.an5.100:5.7: Now that futile man Devadatta will expose himself by his own deeds.
translation_text.an5.100:6.1: Moggallāna, there are these five teachers found in the world.
translation_text.an5.100:6.3: Firstly, some teacher with impure conduct claims: ‘I am pure in ethics. My ethical conduct is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:6.5: ‘This teacher has impure ethical conduct, but claims to be ethically pure.
translation_text.an5.100:6.6: They wouldn’t like it if we were to tell the laypeople.
translation_text.an5.100:6.7: And how could we treat them in a way that they don’t like?
translation_text.an5.100:6.8: But they consent to robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an5.100:6.9: They themselves will be recognized by their own deeds.’
translation_text.an5.100:6.10: The disciples of such a teacher cover up their teacher’s conduct,
translation_text.an5.100:6.11: and the teacher expects them to do so.
translation_text.an5.100:7.1: Furthermore, some teacher with impure livelihood claims: ‘I am pure in livelihood. My livelihood is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:7.3: ‘This teacher has impure livelihood, but claims to have pure livelihood.
translation_text.an5.100:7.4: They wouldn’t like it if we were to tell the laypeople.
translation_text.an5.100:7.5: And how could we treat them in a way that they don’t like?
translation_text.an5.100:7.6: But they consent to robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an5.100:7.7: They themselves will be recognized by their own deeds.’
translation_text.an5.100:7.8: The disciples of such a teacher cover up their teacher’s livelihood,
translation_text.an5.100:7.9: and the teacher expects them to do so.
translation_text.an5.100:8.1: Furthermore, some teacher with impure teaching claims: ‘I am pure in teaching. My teaching is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:8.3: ‘This teacher has impure teaching, but claims to have pure teaching.
translation_text.an5.100:8.4: They wouldn’t like it if we were to tell the laypeople.
translation_text.an5.100:8.5: And how could we treat them in a way that they don’t like?
translation_text.an5.100:8.6: But they consent to robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an5.100:8.7: They themselves will be recognized by their own deeds.’
translation_text.an5.100:8.8: The disciples of such a teacher cover up their teacher’s teaching,
translation_text.an5.100:8.9: and the teacher expects them to do so.
translation_text.an5.100:9.1: Furthermore, some teacher with impure answers claims: ‘I am pure in how I answer. My answers are pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:9.3: ‘This teacher has impure answers, but claims to have pure answers.
translation_text.an5.100:9.4: They wouldn’t like it if we were to tell the laypeople.
translation_text.an5.100:9.5: And how could we treat them in a way that they don’t like?
translation_text.an5.100:9.6: But they consent to robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an5.100:9.7: They themselves will be recognized by their own deeds.’
translation_text.an5.100:9.8: The disciples of such a teacher cover up their teacher’s answers,
translation_text.an5.100:9.9: and the teacher expects them to do so.
translation_text.an5.100:10.1: Furthermore, some teacher with impure knowledge and vision claims: ‘I am pure in knowledge and vision. My knowledge and vision are pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:10.3: ‘This teacher has impure knowledge and vision, but claims to have pure knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an5.100:10.4: They wouldn’t like it if we were to tell the laypeople.
translation_text.an5.100:10.5: And how could we treat them in a way that they don’t like?
translation_text.an5.100:10.6: But they consent to robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an5.100:10.7: They themselves will be recognized by their own deeds.’
translation_text.an5.100:10.8: The disciples of such a teacher cover up their teacher’s knowledge and vision,
translation_text.an5.100:10.9: and the teacher expects them to do so.
translation_text.an5.100:10.10: These are the five teachers found in the world.
translation_text.an5.100:11.1: But Moggallāna, I have pure ethical conduct, and I claim: ‘I am pure in ethical conduct. My ethical conduct is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:11.2: My disciples don’t cover up my conduct, and I don’t expect them to.
translation_text.an5.100:11.3: I have pure livelihood, and I claim: ‘I am pure in livelihood. My livelihood is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:11.4: My disciples don’t cover up my livelihood, and I don’t expect them to.
translation_text.an5.100:11.5: I have pure teaching, and I claim: ‘I am pure in teaching. My teaching is pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:11.6: My disciples don’t cover up my teaching, and I don’t expect them to.
translation_text.an5.100:11.7: I have pure answers, and I claim: ‘I am pure in how I answer. My answers are pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:11.8: My disciples don’t cover up my answers, and I don’t expect them to.
translation_text.an5.100:11.9: I have pure knowledge and vision, and I claim: ‘I am pure in knowledge and vision. My knowledge and vision are pure, bright, uncorrupted.’
translation_text.an5.100:11.10: My disciples don’t cover up my knowledge and vision, and I don’t expect them to.”

variant_text.an5.100:5.5: Rakkhassetaṁ, moggallāna, vācaṁ … → etthantare pāṭho si, sya-all, km, pts1ed potthakesu natthi; pli-tv-kd17:2.1.1 pana sabbatthapi
variant_text.an5.100:6.7: kathaṁ naṁ → kathannu (bj); kathaṁ nu (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr); kathaṁ taṁ (katthaci)
